top_headline: WARSH DAY / CPI EVE — Senate cloture 3pm ET; Hormuz frays, Brent +3.2% $104; CPI tomorrow 3.7%; AMD ATH $455; NVDA May 20
chapters: I · MACRO — Warsh Senate cloture vote 3pm ET; confirmation expected this week; Powell exits May 15; CPI April tomorrow 3.7% YoY est (from 3.3%); Core 2.7%
II · AI TRADE — NVDA May 20 preview: $78.8B est +79% / $1.77 EPS; hyperscaler capex $725B +77%; AMD ATH $455.19; AKAM +26% $1.8B AI cloud deal
III · EARNINGS — Honor Roll: AKAM +26%, INOD +51%, AMD ATH, DIS +6.9%, UBER +10%, APP +59%, SHOP +34%, ARM record / Movers: FWRD -44%, HUBS -23%
IV · GEOPOLITICS & CAPITAL — Hormuz ceasefire frays (US fires on tankers, Iran fires at UAE, Brent +3.2% $104.49); US-UK deal signed May 8; Trump-Xi summit May 14-15 Beijing
V · TRACK RECORD — No-003 (4 May) setups: AMD +26.3% WIN / PLTR -4.4% LOSS / BRENT -5.9% LOSS / 1W 2L / avg +5.3%

flagged_setups.thesis: May 20 Q1 FY2027 earnings preview. Revenue consensus $78.8B +78.6% YoY; EPS $1.77 +118.5% YoY — in line with management guidance of $78B. Hyperscalers (MSFT/AMZN/GOOGL/META) collectively guided ~$725B capex for 2026 (+77% vs 2025). Goldman Sachs: 'major re-rating' likely; expects beat-and-raise. AMD ATH $455 sets a high bar for AI capex delivery. NVDA forward P/E ~25x — compressed vs AI-era average. 3-condition entry: revenue beat >$78B + DC GPU demand commentary strong + China exclusion confirmed not worsening. Risk: stock historically falls more often post-earnings than rises even on beats; AMD set very high bar; Trump-Xi summit May 14-15 could escalate export controls.
flagged_setups.price_note: ~$217 per Bloomberg/Investing.com; ATH was $217.80 on May 8 2026

flagged_setups.thesis: Iran ceasefire fraying but Citi targets deal end-May with $85-90 range on Hormuz normalization. Qatar PM mediation active; Iran sent response to US proposal via Pakistan. Current Brent $104.49 (+3.16% May 11) on ceasefire uncertainty — elevated entry point for short. CENTCOM fired on Iranian tankers May 8; Iran fired at UAE. Trump insists ceasefire 'intact.' Deal thesis: end-May resolution, shipping flows normalize over weeks, supply normalization takes 4-8 weeks from deal to Brent repricing. Risk: ceasefire collapse = spike to $115+; structural if deal fails; Warsh's inflation problem gets much worse.
flagged_setups.price_note: $104.49 Brent crude futures May 11 2026 +3.16% (CNBC oil prices today May 11)

watchlist_hits.event: May 20 Q1 FY2027 earnings approaching; consensus $78.8B +79%; hyperscaler capex $725B; Goldman beat-and-raise call; AMD ATH $455 sets precedent
New ATH $455.19 on May 8 — continued post-earnings momentum from $10.25B Q1 +38% beat; DC $5.8B +57%; 8 analyst upgrades
Valuation wall confirmed — $137.80 as of May 10; structurally unable to hold above $147 despite fundamental beats; ~200x fwd PE ceiling
Q1 report + $1.8B 7-year AI cloud commitment from frontier model provider; Cloud Infrastructure +40% YoY; AI inference validation

tase_events.event: TASE Ltd (TASE.TA) reports Q1 2026 results tomorrow May 12 after market close (conference call 8pm Israeli time). Market cap 15.29B ILS +2.24% last week. Iran ceasefire uncertainty and Brent bounce $104.49 negative for Israeli energy importers; positive for defense sector demand. TA-35 directional watch tied to Warsh/CPI risk and Hormuz resolution.

narrative_threads: Warsh inheritance locked: Fed Chair transition T-4 days; Warsh receives PCE +4.5%, CPI tomorrow 3.7% est, ISM Prices 84.6%, Brent bouncing above $100 — oil inflation impulse that was supposed to dissipate is re-accelerating; zero rate cuts in 2026 now consensus

Hormuz deal timing risk: Iran MOU expected end-May per Citi; but active skirmishes (US fires on tankers, Iran fires at UAE) suggest deal is not imminent; each week of delay = another month of elevated CPI; this is the core tension for Warsh's first decision

AMD vs NVDA resolution: AMD delivered ATH +26.3% from May 4 flag; NVDA still waiting for May 20 catalyst; AMD set the bar very high for AI DC revenue; if NVDA misses or guides light, the AI capex narrative gets complicated — but Goldman and hyperscaler $725B spending suggest beat-and-raise

PLTR valuation ceiling: Three post-earnings selloffs in a row despite fundamental beats; $135-140 appears to be institutional bid floor; $147-150 is structural ceiling; this pattern is now well-established

Trade architecture week: US-UK signed, Trump-Xi May 14-15; two deals in one week would be historically significant; EU still without framework; China Section 301 investigations create escalation risk even as summit signals engagement

today_calendar.event: Warsh cloture vote — Senate floor (full confirmation vote expected later this week)
No major S&P 500 earnings today; 220 reports scheduled across indices
